title: Migration: add staging_verified_by and staging_verified_at to features
spec:
Create a new Supabase migration file that adds two nullable columns to the `features` table:

```sql
ALTER TABLE features
  ADD COLUMN staging_verified_by text DEFAULT NULL,
  ADD COLUMN staging_verified_at timestamptz DEFAULT NULL;
```

File should be placed at `supabase/migrations/` with the next sequential timestamp prefix (check existing files for the naming pattern).

No index needed — these fields are only queried on low-volume completed/unpromoted features.

Do NOT run `supabase db push` — just create the migration file.
acceptance_tests:
Given the migration file exists in supabase/migrations/
When the migration is applied
Then the features table has a staging_verified_by column of type text, nullable, defaulting to NULL
And the features table has a staging_verified_at column of type timestamptz, nullable, defaulting to NULL

title: RLS: verify or add UPDATE policy for features table
spec:
Check `supabase/migrations/` for the existing RLS policies on the `features` table.

Look for an UPDATE policy that allows authenticated clients to update features scoped to their company_id.

If one exists and is correct, no action needed — just note it in a comment or PR description.

If it is missing, create a new migration file that adds:
```sql
CREATE POLICY "users can update own company features"
  ON features FOR UPDATE
  USING (company_id = (SELECT company_id FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid()))
  WITH CHECK (company_id = (SELECT company_id FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid()));
```

Adapt the exact policy to match the pattern used in existing SELECT/INSERT policies on the same table — look at how they scope to company_id.
acceptance_tests:
Given the features table has RLS enabled
When an authenticated user attempts to UPDATE a feature row where company_id matches their company
Then the update is permitted by RLS
And when company_id does not match
Then the update is rejected by RLS

title: WebUI Read: display staging verification badge on features
spec:
Update the WebUI to fetch and display staging verification state on features.

1. Find all `supabase.from('features').select(...)` calls in the WebUI (check `packages/webui/src/`). Add `staging_verified_by` and `staging_verified_at` to those select strings wherever features are fetched for the detail view and completed-features list.

2. Update the TypeScript feature type/interface to include:
   - `staging_verified_by: string | null`
   - `staging_verified_at: string | null`

3. In the feature detail view and completed-features list components, add a verification badge:
   - When `staging_verified_by` is not null: show a green badge reading "Verified by {name} · {relative time}" (e.g. use `date-fns` `formatDistanceToNow` if already available, or a simple fallback)
   - When `staging_verified_by` is null: show nothing (or a subtle grey "Unverified" label if the design calls for it)

4. Export or co-locate the badge as a reusable component — the write job will use it for optimistic updates.
acceptance_tests:
Given a feature with staging_verified_by = 'chris' and staging_verified_at set
When the feature detail or completed-features list is rendered
Then a green badge is visible showing 'Verified by chris' and a relative timestamp

Given a feature with staging_verified_by = NULL
When the feature detail or completed-features list is rendered
Then no green badge is shown

Given the feature type definition
Then it includes staging_verified_by: string | null and staging_verified_at: string | null

Given the verification state persists in the database
When the page is reloaded
Then the verification badge state matches what is stored

title: WebUI Write: mark/unmark staging verification on completed features
spec:
Add the interactive verification toggle to the WebUI.

1. In the feature detail view (and completed-features list if applicable), conditionally render a "Mark verified on staging" button when:
   - `feature.status === 'complete'`
   - AND `feature.promoted_version == null` (or the equivalent field — check the feature type)
   - AND `feature.staging_verified_by == null`

2. On button click:
   - Prompt the user for the verifier name. Use a sensible default (e.g. look for a current user name from the auth session, fall back to `'human'`)
   - Execute: `supabase.from('features').update({ staging_verified_by: name, staging_verified_at: new Date().toISOString() }).eq('id', featureId)`
   - Optimistically update local state so the green badge (from the Read job) appears immediately without waiting for a refetch

3. On the green badge, render a small "×" button:
   - Only visible when the feature is still complete+unpromoted (same conditions as the mark button)
   - On click, execute: `supabase.from('features').update({ staging_verified_by: null, staging_verified_at: null }).eq('id', featureId)`
   - Optimistically clear the badge and re-show the "Mark verified on staging" button

4. Do NOT show the mark button or the × un-verify button on:
   - Features with `promoted_version != null`
   - Features with `status !== 'complete'`
acceptance_tests:
Given a feature with status='complete' and promoted_version=null and staging_verified_by=null
When the feature is rendered
Then a 'Mark verified on staging' button is visible

Given the 'Mark verified on staging' button is clicked and a name entered
When the update completes
Then staging_verified_by and staging_verified_at are set on the feature record in the database
And the green badge appears immediately (optimistic update)

Given a feature showing the green verification badge
When the × button on the badge is clicked
Then staging_verified_by and staging_verified_at are set to NULL in the database
And the badge is replaced by the 'Mark verified on staging' button immediately

Given a feature with promoted_version != null
When the feature is rendered
Then the 'Mark verified on staging' button is NOT shown
And the × un-verify button is NOT shown

Given a feature with status != 'complete'
When the feature is rendered
Then the 'Mark verified on staging' button is NOT shown
